summary: Sanity-grep raw/ for 'book-content' and 'bbook' class collisions before editing selectors
description:
Before editing generic_html.py:

grep -r 'book-content\|"bbook"\|class="bbook' raw/ 2>/dev/null | head

Confirm neither class appears in any existing raw/ source's HTML capture. If 'book-content' appears in unrelated sources (it's a fairly generic name), the simple selector addition risks misclassifying their content. 'bbook' is CCEL-specific and should be safe.

If collisions found: scope the selector match more narrowly (e.g., only when host is ccel.org) before proceeding to c2_2.
